AND 1030 POLK ASSOCIATES' COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST ATTACH THE POLICY OR POLICIES AT ISSUE OR PLEAD MORE SPECIFICALLY THE COVERAGE AT ISSUE. PLAINTIFF ALSO MUST PLEAD SEPARATE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FIRST PARTY AND THIRD PARTY LIABILITY. THE FRAUD CLAIM LACKS THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. THE REFORMATION CLAIM FAILS TO PLEAD MUTUAL MISTAKE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE PLTFS SUBMITTED IN OPPOS OTHSB'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES(4TH-6TH); POS FILED BY DEFENDANT THE HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT RE: 4TH THRU 6TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. (THIS TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT BEING ARGUED.) THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANTS MOTION SEEKS ADVISORY OPINION REGARDING POLICY INTERPRETATION THAT DO NOT RESOLVE ANY LEGAL OR FACTUAL DISPUTES REGARDING THE APPLICABILITY OF COVERAGE OR DEFENDANT'S DUTY TO PAY OR NOT TO PAY UNDER VARIOUS PROVISIONS. DEFENDANT DOES NOT PRESENT EVIDENCE SUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF THAT THE REPLACED ELECTRICAL SERVICE WAS NOT COVERED BECAUSE IT WAS OF A BETTER KIND OR QUALITY THAN REQUIRED. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED REGARDING WHAT THE LAWS WERE BEFORE THE 2004 REPLACEMENT, OR IN THE INTERIM BETWEEN THE 2004 AND 2006 REPLACEMENT. NOTICE OF VIOLATION SUMMARIZES THE VIOLATION AS: "UNLAWFUL USE OF ENERGY....UNSAFE AND HAZARDOUS WIRING." NO EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT THE ORDINANCE OR LAW EXCLUSION APPLIES. THE 6TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS DENIED AS DEFENDANT MERELY SEEKS AN ADVISORY OPINION REGARDING THE CONTRACT. THIS IS NOT AN ISSUE OR DUTY THAT CAN BE SUMMARILY ADJUDICATED. CCP SECTION 437C. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT THE HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED AS TO INTERROGATORY NOS. 4 TO ASIAN AND NO. 6 TO 1030 POLK AS THE RESPONSES ARE INCOMPLETE. THE RESPONSES AND OPPOSITION ARE NOT COMPLETE AS ATTORNEY'S FEES WERE NOT THE ONLY EXTRA-CONTRACTUAL DAMAGES REFERENCED IN THE RESPONSES. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NO. 12 TO ASIAN AND 17 TO 1030 POLK. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NO. 8 TO 1030 POLK AS THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER. FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories
Off Calendar Apr-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Andrew B. Downs, Esq., for defendant and moving party: HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE
Kim Mayor, Esq., and James Boedecker, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Asian Inc. and 1030 Polk Associates

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT THE HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE's Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories is granted as to Interrogatory Nos. 4 to Asian and No. 6 to 1030 Polk as the responses are incomplete. The responses and opposition are not complete as attorney's fees were not the only extra-contractual damages referenced in the responses. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further.
The motion is granted as to No. 12 to Asian and 17 to 1030 Polk. The motion is granted as to No. 8 to 1030 Polk as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT RE: claim for punitive damages. Appearances: Andrew B. Downs, Esq. of Bullivant Houser Bailey PC (415) 352-2716, appearing for Defendant Hartford Steam Boiler Inspection and Insurance Company and Moving party; James Boedecker, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 381-2493 appearing for Plaintiffs; Charles A. Phillips, Esq. of Grimm, Vranjes, McCormick & Graham (619) 231-8802 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants North American. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied, failure to shift the burden of proof to show that plaintiff lacks and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence of oppression, fraud, or malice. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT re: 4th thru 6th affirmative defenses. Appearances: Andrew B. Downs, Esq. of Bullivant Houser Bailey PC (415) 352-2716, appearing for Defendant Hartford Steam Boiler Inspection and Insurance Company and Moving party; James Boedecker, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 381-2493 appearing for Plaintiffs; Charles A. Phillips, Esq. of Grimm, Vranjes, McCormick & Graham (619) 231-8802 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants North American. (This tentative ruling is not being argued.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendants motion seeks advisory opinion regarding policy interpretation that do not resolve any legal or factual disputes regarding the applicability of coverage or defendant's duty to pay or not to pay under various provisions. Defendant does not present evidence sufficient to shift the burden of proof that the replaced electrical service was not covered because it was of a better kind or quality than required. Insufficient evidence presented regarding what the laws were before the 2004 replacement, or in the interim between the 2004 and 2006 replacement. Notice of violation summarizes the violation as: "unlawful use of energy....unsafe and hazardous wiring." No evidence presented that the Ordinance or Law exclusion applies. The 6th affirmative defense is denied as defendant merely seeks an advisory opinion regarding the contract. This is not an issue or duty that can be summarily adjudicated. CCP section 437c. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS ASIAN, INC, and, 1030 POLK ASSOCIATES MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Andrew B. Downs, Esq. of Bullivant Houser Bailey PC (415) 352-2716, appearing for Defendant Hartford Steam Boiler Inspection and Insurance Company; James Boedecker, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 381-2493 appearing for Plaintiffs and Moving party; Charles A. Phillips, Esq. of Grimm, Vranjes, McCormick & Graham (619) 231-8802 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants North American. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied, failure to comply with CRC 3.1350 which requires each issue to be separately stated. Motion denied as to merits, failure to shift burden of proof. No evidence submitted establishing what expenses were necessary relocation costs. Insufficient affidavits, declarations, admissions, or answers to interrogatories submitted to support the motion. CCP section 437c(b)(1) and 437c(p)(1). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Hearing Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of Deft Nonprofits Insurance Alliance Of California'S Memorandum Of P/A In Support Of Its Demurrer To Asian Inc. And 1030 Polk Associates' Complaint;
Off Calendar Aug-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark G. Bonino, Esq. of HAYES, DAVIS (650-486-2869), appearing for defendant /moving party Nonprofits Insurance Alliance of California

Ruling:
DEFENDANT NONPROFITS INSURANCE ALLIANCE of California's Demurrer To Asian Inc. And 1030 Polk Associates' Complaint:
No other appearances on this matter. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT NONPROFITS INSURANCE ALLIANCE of California'S Demurrer To Asian Inc. And 1030 Polk Associates' Complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 1st, 2nd and 6th causes of action. Plaintiff must attach the policy or policies at issue or plead more specifically the coverage at issue. Plaintiff also must plead separate causes of action for first party and third party liability. The fraud claim lacks the requisite specificity. The reformation claim fails to plead mutual mistake. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/VC)
